bios: Survived six winters in the CVS trenches. Flinches violently at any sound resembling a drive-thru alert.
Cures Acute Insurance Rejection Syndrome. Dispels prior authorization blockers with professionalism, and occasionally, violence.
Specialty drug enthusiast. Handles $15,000 biologics with the delicate care of a bomb squad technician.
Escaped the Corner of Happy and Hell. Can count to 90 by fives in their sleep while actively administering immunizations.
The Hieroglyphics Translator. Converts doctor scribbles and hostile shorthand into flawless standard sigs.
Guardian of the cold-chain. Will literally bite you if the fridge door stays open for more than four seconds.
Purple Heart recipient from the Great Flu Shot Offensive. Never leaves a script behind.
Spots suspicious out-of-state prescriptions from across the room. Absolute steel trap.
Escaped through a Walgreens drive-thru window and walked straight here. Their eyes have seen things.
Cures Severe Polypharmacy Blindness with a single authoritative stroke of their red pen.
Former retail manager. Deeply suspicious when allowed to sit down for a full lunch break.
Verifies $20,000 auto-injectors. Heart rate never spikes. Forehead sweats actual ice water.
Eradicates Phantom Refill Syndrome before it infects the active billing queue.
Certified in high-stakes retail drive-thru de-escalation. Only has three fingers on their left hand.
Chief Temperature Officer. Will drop everything and sprint to the thermostat if indoor ambient temperature breaches 65F.
Certified in high-stakes PBM phone tree navigation. Interrogates insurance helpdesks for sport.
Veterinary specialty wizard. Translates a cat's weight into a flawless compounded liquid dose.
Wrote the manual on DUR survival. Can enter override codes blindfolded while riding a horse.
Master of Ice-Pack Tetris. Deeply believes birds aren't real. Harbors an unyielding distrust of all logistics networks.
Can unearth a single canceled order from a mountain of outgoing packages three seconds before the USPS driver arrives.
Plays the tape gun like a musical instrument. Dispenses packing tape at exactly 120 beats per minute.
Can assemble six boxes simultaneously while scream-singing the entire lyrics to "Uptown Funk".
Balances frozen gel packs like a professional juggler. Their hands are permanently 40 degrees Fahrenheit.
Operates entirely by echolocation in the warehouse. Memorizes the exact physical coordinates of every outgoing tote.
Can identify the exact weight of a package just by looking at it. Accurate to within half an ounce.
The Heavy Box Whisperer. Tapes 40-pound bulk specialty orders shut with a single hand while drinking a Monster Energy.
